factors.summary: The company is facing general economic uncertainty and volatility.

evidence.text: volatility and uncertainty in general economic conditions and the pricing environment

factors.summary: There have been no material changes in the Company’s risk factors from those disclosed in Part I, Item 1a. Risk Factors of the Company’s Form 10-K.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes in the Company’s risk factors from those disclosed in Part I, Item 1a. Risk Factors of the Company’s Form 10-K.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable as the company is managing it effectively.

evidence.text: The Company currently expects to invest approximately 1.5% of net sales in capital expenditures during fiscal 2018. For the first nine months of fiscal 2018, $32.2 million had been invested, primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, and new and enhanced information technology capabilities.

factors.summary: Input costs are manageable as the company is experiencing increased wages and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin decreased 120 basis points to 41.3% for the three months ended May 31, 2018 compared with 42.5% in the prior-year period. Gross profit margin was lower than the prior-year period due primarily to unfavorable price/mix, increased wages, and additional reserves for excess inventory related to the closure of a facility, partially offset by higher sales volumes, productivity improvements, and lower quality costs.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: Product quality and safety incidents are not mentioned as significant issues.

evidence.text: The Company is one of the world’s leading providers of lighting and building management solutions and services for commercial, institutional, industrial, infrastructure, and residential applications throughout North America and select international markets. The Company’s lighting and building management solutions include devices such as luminaires, lighting controls, controllers for various building systems, power supplies, prismatic skylights, and drivers, as well as integrated systems designed to optimize energy efficiency and comfort for various indoor and outdoor applications.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving due to strong operating cash flows and capital management.

evidence.text: The Company generated $300.7 million of cash flow from operating activities during the nine months ended May 31, 2018 compared with $198.0 million in the prior-year period, an increase of $102.7 million, due primarily to lower operating working capital requirements, reduced variable incentive payments for prior year performance, and decreased payments for income taxes.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is stable with recent acquisitions supporting growth.

evidence.text: On May 1, 2018, using cash on hand and borrowings available under existing credit arrangements, the Company acquired IOTA Engineering, LLC. On February 12, 2018, using cash on hand, the Company acquired Lucid Design Group, Inc. No acquisitions were completed during fiscal 2017.

factors.summary: Recent acquisitions and new product launches are catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: On May 1, 2018, using cash on hand and borrowings available under existing credit arrangements, the Company acquired IOTA Engineering, LLC. On February 12, 2018, using cash on hand, the Company acquired Lucid Design Group, Inc. ... The Company's IoT solutions provide customers with access to robust data analytics; support the advancement of smart buildings, smart cities, and the smart grid; and allow businesses to develop custom applications to scale their operations.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand, particularly in the infrastructure/utility channel.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales was due primarily to greater shipments of Atrius-based luminaires to customers in certain key vertical applications and increased sales in the infrastructure/utility channel, partially offset by lower shipments for larger commercial projects due to continued tepid conditions within the North American non-residential lighting market.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting growing demand.

The increase in inventory during the nine months ended May 31, 2018 was due primarily to customer expansion in the home center/showroom channel, new product launches, and a buildup of finished goods to support committed projects in the corporate accounts channel. This increase in inventory was mostly offset by a corresponding increase in accounts payable during the same period.

The Company is one of the world’s leading providers of lighting and building management solutions and services for commercial, institutional, industrial, infrastructure, and residential applications throughout North America and select international markets.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing negative pricing conditions, particularly for basic products, driven by increased competition and unfavorable price/mix trends. These factors have negatively impacted net sales and margins.

evidence.text: Unfavorable price/mix reflected changes in both product mix, which included substitutions to certain products with lower price points, and sales channel mix, which included declines in generally higher priced solutions, primarily for commercial projects. Price/mix was also impacted by lower pricing on certain luminaires, reflecting increased competition in more basic, lesser-featured products.
evidence.subsection: First Nine Months of Fiscal 2018 Compared with First Nine Months of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: Unfavorable price/mix reflected changes in both product mix, which included substitutions to certain products with lower price points, and sales channel mix, which included declines in generally higher priced solutions, primarily for larger commercial projects. Price/mix was also impacted by lower pricing on certain luminaires, reflecting increased competition in more basic, lesser-featured products.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: Demand in the North American lighting market is expected to improve modestly in the second half of 2018, though the third quarter showed mixed signals with stable demand despite some weaker areas.

evidence.text: Third-party forecasts and leading indicators suggest that demand in the North American lighting market, the Company’s primary market, will improve modestly in the second half of calendar 2018 following several quarters of weak demand.

Net sales were $944.0 million for the three months ended May 31, 2018 compared with $891.6 million reported for the three months ended May 31, 2017, an increase of $52.4 million, or 5.9%. ... The increase in net sales was due primarily to greater shipments of Atrius-based luminaires to customers in certain key vertical applications and increased sales in the infrastructure/utility channel, partially offset by lower shipments for larger commercial projects due to continued tepid conditions within the North American non-residential lighting market.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: The company's forward guidance indicates expectations of modest demand improvement and tax benefits, with stable capital expenditure plans. However, there is no updated forward-looking information provided beyond what was previously disclosed.

evidence.text: Except as required by law, the Company undertakes no obligation to publicly update or release any revisions to these forward-looking statements to reflect any events or circumstances after the date of this quarterly report.

Management expects the TCJA [...] to favorably impact the Company’s net income, diluted earnings per share, and cash flows in future periods [...] Management currently estimates that the Company’s blended consolidated effective income tax rate [...] for full-year fiscal 2018 will approximate 26% to 28% before discrete items, compared with nearly 35% for the prior year.

The Company currently expects to invest approximately 1.5% of net sales in capital expenditures during fiscal 2018. ... Management believes that the Company will be able to meet its liquidity needs over the next 12 months based on its cash on hand, current projections of cash flow from operations, and borrowing availability under recently executed financing arrangements.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, driven by increased sales of basic products and changes in sales channels, as well as increased competition.

evidence.text: Unfavorable price/mix reflected changes in both product mix, which included substitutions to certain products with lower price points, and sales channel mix, which included declines in generally higher priced solutions, primarily for commercial projects.

evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to higher selling, distribution, and administrative expenses as well as margin pressure.

evidence.text: The increase in SD&A expenses was primarily due to higher employee related costs, including additional headcount from acquisitions, increased freight charges and commissions to support greater sales volume, higher professional fees related to recent acquisitions, and to a lesser degree, certain other operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: First Nine Months of Fiscal 2018 Compared with First Nine Months of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: The increase in SD&A expenses was due primarily to higher employee related costs, including additional headcount from acquisitions, increased freight charges and commissions to support greater sales volume, and higher professional fees related to recent acquisitions.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum for the North American non-residential lighting market is mixed. While the infrastructure/utility and certain vertical applications are growing, the market overall is experiencing tepid conditions, particularly impacting larger commercial projects.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales was due primarily to greater shipments of Atrius-based luminaires to customers in certain key vertical applications and increased sales in the infrastructure/utility channel, partially offset by lower shipments through the home center/showroom sales channel and certain international channels as well as for larger commercial projects due to continued tepid conditions within the North American non-residential lighting market.
evidence.subsection: First Nine Months of Fiscal 2018 Compared with First Nine Months of Fiscal 2017

evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with evidence pointing to weak demand and unfavorable price/mix, particularly affecting larger commercial projects and certain international channels. However, there are areas of growth, such as increased shipments of Atrius-based luminaires and higher sales in the infrastructure/utility channel.

evidence.text: Net sales were $2.62 billion for the nine months ended May 31, 2018 compared with $2.55 billion reported for the nine months ended May 31, 2017, an increase of $71.4 million, or 2.8%. factors.summary: The company is pursuing cost reduction programs, such as facility consolidations and workforce reductions, which are expected to generate savings. However, these savings are offset by investments in growth opportunities, and the impact of the cost-cutting measures is currently limited.

evidence.text: Management expects to incur additional costs in future periods associated with the closing of facilities, primarily attributable to early lease termination costs and relocation costs. Annual savings realized from the streamlining activities, once fully completed, are expected to exceed the amount of the special charge and will be reinvested in activities to support higher-growth opportunities as well as drive improved profitability.

The Company is investing in assets and programs that will over time increase the overall return on its invested capital. The Company invested $32.2 million and $55.2 million in the first nine months of fiscal 2018 and 2017, respectively, primarily related to investments in new equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, and information technology.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a negative margin trajectory, with both gross and operating margins compressing across the first nine months and third quarter of fiscal 2018 compared to the same periods in fiscal 2017. The compression is attributed to factors including unfavorable price/mix, increased labor costs, excess inventory reserves, pricing pressure, higher costs, and increased expenses.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin decreased to 41.0% for the nine months ended May 31, 2018 compared with 42.2% in the prior-year period. [...] The decrease in operating profit was due primarily to an increase in SD&A expenses and a higher special charge.
evidence.subsection: First Nine Months of Fiscal 2018 Compared with First Nine Months of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: Gross profit margin decreased 120 basis points to 41.3% for the three months ended May 31, 2018 compared with 42.5% in the prior-year period. Operating profit decreased by $25.6 million, or 19.5%, primarily due to the impact of price/mix on gross profit, higher SD&A expenses, and increased special charges.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: Management exhibits mixed confidence, expressing cautious optimism about long-term strategic growth while facing short-term challenges. Their consistent dividend payments and share repurchases indicate confidence in their strategy and execution.

evidence.text: Management continues to believe the execution of the Company's strategy will provide attractive opportunities for profitable growth over the long-term. [...] Recent softness in the lighting industry has created a challenging environment for management to deliver financial performance in the short term while, at the same time, continuing to invest in attractive longer-term opportunities.

Acuity Brands paid dividends on its common stock of $16.2 million and $17.2 million ( $0.39 per share) during the nine months ended May 31, 2018 and 2017, respectively. ... The Company expects to repurchase the remaining shares available for repurchase on an opportunistic basis. The Company has repurchased a total of 2.0 million shares in fiscal 2018.

factors.summary: The company faces several near-term risks, including challenging pricing conditions, increased competition, ongoing restructuring costs, and potential impacts from U.S. trade policies. These factors could negatively affect net sales and margins, as well as influence business and consumer sentiment.

evidence.text: Management expects the pricing environment to continue to be challenging in portions of the market, particularly for more basic, lesser-featured products sold through certain sales channels as well as shifts in product mix, both of which are expected to continue to negatively impact net sales and margins.

Any future policy changes that may be implemented could have a positive or negative consequence on the Company’s financial performance depending on how the changes would influence many factors, including business and consumer sentiment.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: The unfavorable price/mix reflected changes in both product mix, which included substitutions to certain products with lower price points, and sales channel mix, which included declines in generally higher priced solutions, primarily for larger commercial projects.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: Price/mix was also impacted by lower pricing on certain luminaires, reflecting increased competition in more basic, lesser-featured products.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy emphasizes expanding its lighting and building management solutions, particularly through integrated, tiered approaches in emerging markets. Additionally, a focus on IoT solutions and robust infrastructure supports the advancement of smart buildings and cities, indicating a well-resourced and comprehensive strategic direction.

evidence.text: The Company's strategy is to capitalize on market growth and share gain opportunities by continuing to expand and leverage its industry-leading lighting and building management solutions portfolio [...] to capture market share in the nascent, but rapidly growing, market for data capture, analytics, and other services, transforming buildings and campuses from cost centers to strategic assets.

The Company's IoT solutions provide customers with access to robust data analytics; support the advancement of smart buildings, smart cities, and the smart grid; and allow businesses to develop custom applications to scale their operations. As of May 31, 2018, the Company operates 20 manufacturing facilities and seven distribution facilities along with three warehouses to serve its extensive customer base.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While there is increased borrowing capacity to support stock repurchases, indicating confidence in future growth, the capital expenditures remain stable with consistent investments in equipment and facilities.

evidence.text: The increase in borrowing capacity provides the Company with the resources to accommodate the current stock repurchase program of which 5.2 million shares remain available for repurchase. [...] The Company may increase its leverage to accommodate the stock repurchase program.

The Company currently expects to invest approximately 1.5% of net sales in capital expenditures during fiscal 2018. For the first nine months of fiscal 2018, $32.2 million had been invested, primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, and new and enhanced information technology capabilities.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed sensitivities across various factors, including U.S. tax and trade policies, competition, input costs, and market demand conditions. While policy changes could impact financial performance, increased competition has led to pricing pressures and changes in product mix, affecting profitability.

evidence.text: The company is sensitive to changes in U.S. tax policy, trade policies, and market demand in its primary North American market.

Notwithstanding the TCJA, a great amount of rhetoric and debate remains regarding a wide range of policy options with respect to monetary, regulatory, and trade, amongst others, that the U.S. federal government has and may pursue [...] Any future policy changes that may be implemented could have a positive or negative consequence on the Company’s financial performance depending on how the changes would influence many factors, including business and consumer sentiment.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

evidence.text: The unfavorable price/mix reflected changes in both product mix, which included substitutions to certain products with lower price points, and sales channel mix, which included declines in generally higher priced solutions, primarily for larger commercial projects. Price/mix was also impacted by lower pricing on certain luminaires, reflecting increased competition in more basic, lesser-featured products.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2018 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2017

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage has shown slight stability with minimal changes in debt levels, though there is evidence of increased borrowing capacity to support a stock repurchase program, indicating confidence in managing debt and investing in growth.

evidence.text: The Company entered into a new credit agreement [...] that increased the Company’s borrowing capacity [...] provides the Company with the resources to accommodate the current stock repurchase program.

Total debt outstanding was $358.0 million and $356.9 million at May 31, 2018 and August 31, 2017, respectively, and consisted primarily of fixed-rate obligations. ... The Company’s debt to total capitalization ratio was 18.3% and 17.6% at May 31, 2018 and August 31, 2017, respectively.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are stable with consistent dividend payments and share repurchases, reflecting the company's confidence in its future performance.

evidence.text: The Company may increase its leverage to accommodate the stock repurchase program of which 5.2 million shares remain available for repurchase.
Acuity Brands paid dividends on its common stock of $16.2 million and $17.2 million ( $0.39 per share) during the nine months ended May 31, 2018 and 2017, respectively.
The Company expects to repurchase the remaining shares available for repurchase on an opportunistic basis. The Company has repurchased a total of 2.0 million shares in fiscal 2018.

factors.summary: The company is managing inventory levels with reserves for excess inventory due to facility closures, while also seeing an increase in inventory levels because of customer expansion and new product launches.

evidence.text: The Company also recorded a $3.1 million charge during the third quarter of fiscal 2018 to reserve for raw material inventory located at one of the facilities where production activities will cease because it would not be cost effective to relocate that inventory.

The increase in inventory during the nine months ended May 31, 2018 was due primarily to customer expansion in the home center/showroom channel, new product launches, and a buildup of finished goods to support committed projects in the corporate accounts channel. This increase in inventory was mostly offset by a corresponding increase in accounts payable during the same period.
